meta.name: Department of Agriculture
meta.description: govOS free public service: a plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 22 (Department of Agriculture) — the voter-established department, its food-security mission, agricultural parks, and ties to ag zoning. Sourced to Municode + County; first version, expanding.
meta.pageTitle: Title 22 — Department of Agriculture · govOS · Kilo Aupuni
meta.eyebrow: 12 Stones Global · Kilo Aupuni · govOS · public service

hero.lead: A plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 22 (Department of Agriculture) — the department Maui voters established in November 2020 to build a sustainable local food system. This title covers the department's mission and the County's agricultural parks (e.g. Kula). It pairs with the Title 19 Agricultural district (Ch. 19.30A), which is where ag land use is regulated. Sections marked expanding are being filled in; nothing here is legal advice.

sections.title: 1 · The Department of Agriculture
sections.note: Why the department exists and what it's charged to do.
sections.html: Maui County voters approved the Department of Agriculture in November 2020 (a charter amendment), making Maui one of the few counties with a dedicated ag department. Its charge: develop a sustainable regional agricultural system, build the economic resiliency of farming communities, and strengthen resident health and food security through access to locally grown products. The department reports annually to the mayor and Council on the County's progress and on barriers that could be fixed by ordinance.

source.html: County of Maui Department of Agriculture + MCC Title 22.

sections.title: 2 · Agricultural parks (e.g. Kula)
sections.note: How the County puts land directly into farmers' hands.
sections.html: Title 22 includes the County's agricultural parks program (e.g. Kula Agricultural Park, Ch. 22.04A). Its purpose is to promote diversified agriculture by offering appropriately sized ag lots at reasonable rent with long-term tenure — lowering the biggest barrier for working farmers: secure, affordable access to land. This is the County acting directly to grow local food, complementing the zoning rules in Title 19.

source.html: Maui County Code Title 22, Ch. 22.04A (Kula Agricultural Park), Municode.
source.expanding: park lot availability, rent schedule & application

sections.title: 3 · How it connects to ag zoning (Title 19)
sections.note: Title 22 is the department & land; Title 19 is the land-use rule.
sections.html: Farming on Maui touches two titles: Title 22 (this one) is the Department of Agriculture and its parks, while Title 19's Agricultural district (Ch. 19.30A) sets what you can build and do on ag land — permitted and special uses, farm dwellings, the farm-plan requirements, and the 2-acre minimum lot. If you farm, read both: the Title 19 service covers the ag use rules in detail.

source.html: Maui County Code Ch. 19.30A (Agricultural district) (sourced in the Title 19 service) + Title 22.

sections.title: 4 · The lens — food sovereignty
sections.note: Labeled analysis — agriculture read through two charters. Not adopted law; an interpretive lens beside the Code.
sections.mauiCharter: Under the Maui County Charter (as amended by the 2020 vote), the Department of Agriculture is charged with building a sustainable regional food system and reporting annually on progress and barriers. The Charter frames agriculture as an economic-development and food-security function of County government.
sections.sovereignCharter: Under the Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) lens, the ability to feed one's people from one's own land is the root of sovereignty — ʻāina momona (abundant land). The lens reads the department and its ag parks as restoring food sovereignty: land to farmers, local food to residents, and resilience against import dependence.

position.label: ⚖️ Position — analysis, not law
position.text: Land in farmers' hands and food on residents' tables is sovereignty made real. The department's job is to remove barriers — secure tenure, affordable land, fewer permit hurdles — so Maui can feed Maui. Same title, read two ways.

sections.text: Free now (no account): this whole page is Tier 1 — free. Coming — Tier 3 (verified tools): an ag-park interest helper and an AI agriculture assistant (linking Title 22 programs with Title 19 farm-plan rules) will be verification-gated via the free govOS beta portal (identity verified with Stripe Identity — free, no charge); only verified-status is stored, never the raw ID. No secret keys (environment references only).
